channel.description: Job listings for Public Affairs roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Head of Advocacy
item.description: The Opportunity ‚Äç Water UK is looking to recruit a Head of Advocacy, a high-profile leadership role with responsibility for shaping and delivering our national advocacy strategy. ‚Äç Reporting to the Director of Public Affairs, the role sitsat the heart of a rapidly growing, influential team and offers regular exposureto senior political figures, CEOs and FTSE-level industry leaders. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead Water UK‚Äôs advocacy strategy, shaping and delivering campaigns that influence national policy and public debate Provide high-quality briefings for senior stakeholders, and represent Water UK externally, including at party conferences, parliamentary meetings and senior stakeholder roundtables Work closely with the CEO, Director of Public Affairs and Head of Public Affairs to ensure advocacy activity aligns with organisational priorities, and collaborate across teams to deliver consistent messaging and strategy Line-manage and develop an Advocacy Manager contributing to a high-performance culture ‚Äç Key Skills & Experience Required ‚Äç Significant experience in advocacy, government relations or similar senior political roles, with a proven track record of shaping and delivering advocacy or influencing strategies Strong political judgement and deep understanding of UK parliamentary processes, with prior knowledge of the water, environmental or infrastructure sectors being desirable (but not essential) Confident working with senior internal and external stakeholders at the highest level, with the ability to handle high expectations Line management or team leadership experience is highly desirable ‚Äç To apply ‚Äç We have partnered with Ellwood Atfield on the appointment ofthis role. If this role is of interest, please apply with your CV and Covering Letter via the link below - we welcome early applications. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Policy Manager
item.description: An established trade within the financial and professional services sector is seeking a Policy Manager to support its commercial finance agenda. The organisation works closely with industry participants, policy makersand regulators to promote a responsible and well-functioning sector that supports UK businesses and the wider economy. With increasing focus on SME access to finance amid economic and regulatory change, this role offers the opportunity to contribute tohigh-profile policy discussions and engage directly with senior stakeholders across industry and government. Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead on policy issues affecting SME access to finance, including areas such as personal guarantees, credit data and sector-specific lending. Draft consultation responses, policy papers, briefings and board materials with minimal oversight. Support research projects, events and wider delivery of the commercial finance policy programme. Coordinate and contribute to member committees, including preparing agendas and minutes. Engage with government, regulators and industry stakeholders through meetings and roundtables. Build relationships with a broad member base, including banks and specialist lenders. Monitor regulatory and legislative developments, identifying implications and engagement opportunities. Produce member communications including updates, articles and presentations. Skills & Experience ‚Äç Excellent written skills and proven ability to write policy reports and consultation documents Previous experience in financial services, policy, government, regulation or a membership body environment. Confident engaging with senior stakeholders and representing organisational positions. Strong analytical and research skills, with the ability to interpret complex regulatory material. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. item.title: Business Development Manager
item.description: The Opportunity: ‚Äç We are partnering with a leading trade and accreditation body to recruit a Business Development Manager. This role focuses on expanding market share by promoting professional training programmes to employers with the capacity to train at scale. You‚Äôll drive new business growth by positioning their brand and qualifications with large or high‚Äëgrowth organisations, identifying fresh opportunities, and converting them into long‚Äëterm training partners. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities: ‚Äç Proactively identify and pursue opportunities to grow the number of clients engaging with our professional training programmes. Map and build relationships with organisations that have the potential to train significant numbers of trainees. Track performance metrics to ensure targets are met and exceeded. ‚Äç Key Skills Required: ‚Äç Proven business development experience, ideally in a B2B environment is essential Experience working in practice, private equity, or corporate finance functions, or strong knowledge of professional services and related sectors is desirable. Strong negotiation and contract management skills. Demonstrated success in lead generation and sales conversion. Excellent skills in Word, Excel and PowerPoint, with confidence using CRM systems. item.title: Interim Head of Public Affairs
item.description: The Opportunity We are partnering with aleading trade association in the financial services sector to recruit an experienced interim Head of Public Affairs to lead their political and parliamentary engagement at a pivotal time for the financial services sector.This is a 6 month fixed term contract, where you will oversee a high-performing team, manage engagement with parliamentarians, and work across the organisation and with senior industry leaders to deliver meaningful impact. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities Lead, mentor and support the public affairs team to foster an inclusive, collaborative culture Develop and deliver the organisation‚Äôs advocacy strategy and political engagement plans Lead engagement with parliament, government and influential stakeholders Shape policy positions in collaboration with internal policy teams Provide strategic political insight, advice and briefings to senior colleagues Manage senior engagement programmes with member firms Represent the organisation at political and policy events, including conferences ‚Äç Skills and ExperienceRequired Strong experience in public affairs, ideally within the financial services sector Experience in managing a team Excellent understanding of parliamentary and government processes Proven ability to build trusted relationships with political stakeholders Experience designing and delivering successful advocacy strategies ‚Äç Right to Work ‚Äç Applicants must have thelegal right to work in the UK. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Social Media and Content Executive
item.description: The organisation ‚Äç A top UK law firm with a strong international presence, recognised for its high-profile client work and distinctive, market-leading brand. The firm operates across key sectors including innovation, private wealth and real estate, with a reputation for excellence in complex and high-stakes matters. It combines deep sector expertise with a creative, client-focused approach. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç This top law firm is seeking a Creative Content Executive to join its agile Communications Team, supporting the delivery of high-quality, engaging digital content. Reporting to the Communications Manager, the Creative Content Executive will focus on social media, campaigns and digital storytelling to strengthen the firm‚Äôs brand and market presence. The role offers the opportunity to work closely with marketing, events, digital and business development teams, as well as stakeholders across the firm. ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç ‚Ä¢ Plan and deliver creative, brand-consistent content and digital campaigns across social media channels including LinkedIn, Instagram and YouTube ‚Ä¢ Manage and grow social media channels, ensuring content is engaging, timely and aligned with wider firm activity ‚Ä¢ Monitor performance and optimise campaigns using analytics tools, providing insight and recommendations ‚Ä¢ Collaborate with internal teams and external agencies to develop content, campaigns and thought leadership ‚Ä¢ Support internal communications and advise colleagues on digital presence, including LinkedIn and video content. ‚Äç Key skills and experience ‚Äç ‚Ä¢ Proven experience in social media, digital content or communications roles ‚Ä¢ Strong understanding of social media platforms, trends, formats and audience engagement ‚Ä¢ Experience creating digital content, including graphics and video, using tools such as Adobe Creative Suite or similar ‚Ä¢ Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to work across teams and stakeholders ‚Ä¢ Strong organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines. ‚Äç Application ‚Äç Please apply below by 2 nd April. ‚Äç

item.title: Account Director / Senior Account Director (Technology)
item.description: The Organisation Our client is a fast-growing, independent communications agency specialising in technology, investment, and purpose-led organisations. Now in its fourth year, the agency has built a strong reputation for working with ‚Äúmeaningful‚Äù organisations across climate, femtech, DEI, financial inclusion, health, and education, alongside some of the most ambitious companies shaping the future of technology. The business has grown revenue by 30% over the last financial year. With a growing international footprint, including expansion into New York, the agency supports a diverse client base spanning start ups,scale ups, investors, and institutions across multiple global markets. Its approach is grounded in thoughtful storytelling, high-quality media engagement, and a genuine respect for journalism. The Opportunity This is a key hire within a high-performing and values-driven agency at a pivotal stage of growth. You will take ownership of key client relationships from the outset, leading delivery across accounts while managing and developing a team. Alongside client work, you will contribute to the broader direction of the business, including input into strategy, operations, and new business activity. The role offers significant scope for impact, responsibility, and progression within a collaborative and intellectually curious environment. Main Responsibilities Lead the strategic direction and delivery of multiple client accounts Build trusted, senior-level relationships with clients across sectors Develop and deliver media strategies that generate high-impact coverage Drive proactive media engagement and maintain strong journalist relationships Manage, mentor, and develop junior team members Contribute to business growth through new business and organic account development Support wider agency initiatives across operations and strategy Skills & Experience Demonstrable experience managing client relationships within a PR agency environment Strong background in technology or technology-adjacent communications Proven ability to secure high-quality, top-tier media coverage Experience leading and developing teams Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong understanding of UK, US, and European media landscapes Interest in purpose, emerging technologies, including AI, and their broader impact Commercial awareness with the ability to identify growth opportunities Wider Benefits One-month paid sabbatical after four years‚Äô service Flexible wellbeing support, including fitness or therapy subsidy Generous annual leave, including additional time over the Christmas period and a birthday day off Regular team activities and company-wide events Monthly development check-ins and access to external training Home office and technology provision Access to learning and wellbeing platforms Health-related benefits including eye care support Close To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles at leadingagencies, contact Tasmin at Tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . item.title: Public Affairs Manager
item.description: The organisation Directors UK is the professional association for UK screen directors, representing over 8,000 members across film and television. As a collective management organisation, it negotiates rights deals and distributes royalties, while also championing the creative and economic interests of directors. Directors UK works closely with government, regulators and industry bodies to influence policy, protect rights and support a thriving and inclusive creative sector. The opportunity Directors UK is seeking a PublicAffairs Manager to support and deliver its public affairs strategy at a pivotal time for the screen industries. Reporting to the Director of Communications andPublic Affairs, the Public Affairs Manager will play a key role in engaging government, parliamentarians and stakeholders on issues including copyright,AI, and the freelance economy. This is a hands-on role in a small, collaborative organisation, offering the opportunity to shape policy activity, build relationships and support high-profile engagement across the creative sector. ‚Äç Main responsibilities Support the delivery of Directors UK‚Äôs publicaffairs strategy, working closely with the Director of Communications and Public Affairs Monitor political and policy developments,providing insight, horizon scanning and recommendations on emerging issues and opportunities Draft briefings, consultation responses and policy submissions, building a strong evidence base to support key priorities Map and engage stakeholders across government, parliament and industry, supporting relationship building and external engagement Work with the communications team to align public affairs activity with wider campaigns, content and stakeholder messaging. Key skills and experience Work with the communications team to align public affairs activity with wider campaigns, content and stakeholder messaging. Experience in a public affairs, government relations or policy role Strong understanding of UK government and parliamentary process Experience drafting briefings, consultation responses and policy documents Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex issues clearly Strong stakeholder management skills and a proactive approach to identifying opportunities. ‚Äç Application Please apply below. The deadline for applications is Wednesday 15 th April. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Public Affairs & Policy Manager (Wales) - Not-For-Profit (Health Sector)
item.description: About the Role ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is partnering with a leading not-for-profit organisation in the health sector to appoint a Public Affairs & Policy Manager. This is a key role leading policy and external affairs activity in Wales, with collaboration across UK-wide teams. ‚Äç The successful candidate will lead public affairs strategy, manage stakeholder relationships, and provide expert political and policy advice to senior leadership. You will oversee a small team and play an important role in shaping the organisation‚Äôs external engagement and policy positioning. ‚Äç This role offers the opportunity to influence health policy, build strong stakeholder relationships, and contribute to meaningful impact across the healthcare system. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç You will lead public affairs and policy activity in Wales, providing strategic insight and advice to internal and external stakeholders. You will build and maintain strong relationships with policymakers, government bodies, and partners across the health sector, ensuring the organisation is positioned as a credible and influential voice. ‚Äç You will provide high-quality political intelligence, policy analysis, briefings, and advice to senior leaders. The role will involve overseeing policy projects, consultations, stakeholder engagement, and external communications activity, while managing and supporting a small team to deliver high-quality work. Working closely with colleagues across the UK, you will contribute to a joined-up external affairs strategy and help ensure the organisation responds effectively to developments in the political and policy environment. ‚Äç ‚Äç About You ‚Äç We are looking for someone with a strong understanding of the Welsh political, public affairs, and health policy environment. You will have experience providing policy and strategic advice to senior stakeholders and be confident in building trusted relationships with senior external partners. ‚Äç You will bring excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce high-quality briefings and policy documents. You will have experience managing complex workstreams and delivering projects to a high standard, as well as the ability to lead or support a team. Welsh language skills are desirable but not essential. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç How to Apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via Ellwood Atfield. Suitable applicants will be invited to an initial briefing call with our team to discuss the role in more detail. ‚Äç Closing date: Monday 27 April 2026 Early applications are strongly encouraged , as shortlisting and interviews will take place on a rolling basis. ‚Äç ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Communications and PR Manager
item.description: ‚Äç ‚Äç About the organisation: ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to partner with the National Centre for Universities and Business (NCUB) who serve as the collective voice of its members, comprising the UK‚Äôs universities and leading businesses. ‚Äç NCUB is committed to bringing organisations together to leverage the skills and talent fostered by UK universities and to further strengthen thenation‚Äôs world-class research and development. Its strategic, cross-sector network links universities, businesses, and governments together to provide a platform for members to engage on areas of shared opportunity. NCUB is a trusted expert, driven by data. ‚Äç Through strong relationships with UKRI and government, NCUB acts as a voice of influence, ensuring its members‚Äô views are heard. Now, more tha never, the UK needs the shared learning and skills that arise from collaboration. This approach will help build a stronger nation, with a flourishing economy, a united society, and lasting positive impact. ‚Äç The opportunity: ‚Äç NCUB are seeking an experiencedCommunications and PR Manager to lead their communications function and strengthen its visibility, reach and influence. This is a strategic and hands-on role, focused on ensuring NCUB‚Äôs work engages key audiences including policymakers, senior leaders across business and higher education, and the media. You will develop a proactive, media-focused approach that raises the organisation‚Äôs profile and positions it as a credible voice in national discussions on innovation, skills and economic growth. ‚Äç Working closely with the SeniorLeadership Team, you will shape messaging, support external engagement and build strong relationships with journalists and stakeholders. You will also oversee the creation of high-quality content across digital and communications channels, ensuring a clear and consistent voice. ‚Äç This is an exciting opportunity to take ownership of a critical function and play a leading role in how a small, ambitious organisation influences thinking and delivers meaningful impact at the heart of the UK‚Äôs innovation ecosystem. ‚Äç Main responsibilities: ‚Äç ‚Äç Lead and deliver NCUB‚Äôs communications strategy to maximise reach, impact and visibility. Develop and implement a proactive PR approach, building strong media and stakeholder relationships. Drive media engagement, securing coverage and thought leadership opportunities while supporting senior leaders. Oversee digital and content channels, using data and AI tools to improve performance and engagement. Manage brand, website and content, evaluate impact, support stakeholder communications and line manage the Events Manager. Line manage three members of staff, including the Donor Relations Manager, Proposition Manager and Prospect Development Manager: coaching and empowering the team to achieve their operational plans and priorities. Cultivate strong ties across the charity to ensure all work delivered by the team is informed by strategic and operational decisions and that colleagues provide the information and access required to deliver high quality relationship management, prospect research, stewardship and propositions. Lead delivery of the operations elements of the Campaign. Ensure the delivery of a compelling and robust case for support, a suite of impactful fundraising materials, a strong prospect pool, a series of cultivation and stewardship events, and a stewardship matrix which can be flexed to meet the needs of different types of donors. Keep up to date with ARUK‚Äôs research and other news to be able to engage effectively with supporters, communicating the charity‚Äôs key messages both among the team and to key supporters. ‚Äç Key skills and experience required: ‚Äç Proven experience delivering strategic communications and PR activity that raises organisational profile and secures impactful media coverage. Strong ability to translate complex issues into clear, compelling messages, with excellent copywriting skills across multiple formats. Demonstrated expertise in using digital, social and traditional channels to drive engagement, alongside data-led evaluation of communications performance. Experience identifying and securing media opportunities, building relationships with journalists, and using monitoring tools to track impact. ‚Äç ‚Äç Benefits: ‚Äç Salary banding: ¬£45,000 - ¬£50,000, dependent on experience Hybrid working (2 days per week in the office) Office based in London (SE1 6FE) 30 days including 3-day Christmas closedown plus Bank Holidays Enhanced Maternity and Paternity pay Employer pension contribution of 5% (on top of the 5% employee contribution) Rolling annual training and development plan Benenden Healthcare scheme. ‚Äç ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‚Äç The deadline for applications is Friday 17 April 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director (Crisis)
item.description: ‚Äç The Organisation ‚Äç Our client is a well-established strategic communications consultancy. They work across a range of sectors, supporting clients through complex and often high-profile situations. ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç This is a senior role within a growing and ambitious practice, offering the opportunity to shape and develop crisis and issues communications capabilities. The successful candidate will play a key role in leading client work, contributing to the broader growth of the practice, and defining areas of specialism within a generalist offer. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead the delivery of crisis and issues communications programmes for a range of clients Provide senior strategic counsel on reputational risk, litigation, and high-profile issues Support the development and positioning of the crisis practice within the wider business Build strong, trusted relationships with clients and key external stakeholders Oversee and develop account teams, ensuring high-quality delivery Contribute to new business activity, including pitches and proposals Maintain high standards across client work, ensuring consistency and efficiency Mentor and support the development of junior colleagues ‚Äç Skills & Experience ‚Äç Strong experience in crisis, issues, and/or litigation communications (essential) Excellent understanding of the media landscape and handling of sensitive situations Proven ability to advise senior stakeholders in complex environments Demonstrated leadership capability with a collaborative approach Commercial awareness, with experience supporting business development activity Ability to operate with sound judgement in fast-paced, high-pressure situations ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package Flexible and hybrid working arrangements Generous leave and wellbeing support Structured learning and development opportunities Inclusive, collaborative culture with regular social and team activities ‚Äç To Apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. item.title: Speechwriter
item.description: Ellwood Atfield is partnering with a leading mission-driven not for profit to find an exceptional Speechwriter. This is a rare opportunity to craft inspiring, high-stakes speeches for a CEO and senior leadership team - translating cutting-edge research and science into narratives that move audiences. ‚Äç You'll bring: ‚Äç Outstanding storytelling skills, making complex science compelling and accessible Ideally a strong portfolio across formats and audiences and speechwriting experience The ability to adapt voice and tone for different leaders and contexts ‚Äç This role sits at the heart of a communications and advocacy team doing work that genuinely matters. ‚Äç Interested? item.title: Research & Policy Executive
item.description: ‚Äç The organisation ‚Äç The Global Infrastructure Investor Association (GIIA) is the leading global body representing private infrastructure investors, fund managers and advisors. Its members manage over $2 trillion across energy, transport, digital and social infrastructure, supporting investment in systems that underpin economic growth, connectivity and the transition to net zero. GIIA operates at the intersection of industry and policy, working with governments and stakeholders to create environments that enable sustainable and resilient infrastructure. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç GIIA is seeking a Research & Policy Executive to join its Policy and Public Affairs team, supporting the development of high-quality research and evidence-led policy work. This is an excellent opportunity to build experience across global markets, contributing to work spanning the UK, Europe and the US. The Research & Policy Executive will play a key role in strengthening GIIA‚Äôs research capability, supporting policy development and engaging with members on key infrastructure issues. ‚Äç ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Conduct research and data analysis on infrastructure policy, investment and market trends across priority regions Monitor political and policy developments, producing clear insight and summaries for internal and external audiences Support the development of evidence-based policy positions, including drafting briefings, reports and consultation responses Maintain and develop internal datasets and evidence bases, ensuring accuracy and analytical rigour Support member engagement through working groups, research projects and policy-focused events. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key skills and experience ‚Äç Degree, or equivalent, in politics, economics, international relations or a related field Strong research and analytical skills, with the ability to synthesise complex information High attention to detail and confidence working with data, including Excel Strong written and verbal communication skills Interest in infrastructure, investment and public policy, with an understanding of political and policy processes. ‚Äç ‚Äç Application ‚Äç Please apply below. ‚Äç

item.title: Head of Consumer Communications
item.description: The Opportunity If you are motivated by scale, visibility, and the chance to shape how a nation engages with a brand,this role offers something genuinely distinctive. ‚Äç This organisationoperates one of the UK‚Äôs most widely recognised consumer products, reachingmillions of people every week through a vast national network. item.title: Events Executive
item.description: About the organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is delighted to have partnered with a respected trade association who are looking for an Events Executive on a permanent basis. The organisation is renowned for working alongside Government and business leaders to provide insights and advice around securing investment opportunities in Asia. The organisation work closely with its members to help businesses influence policy, connect with decision-makers and identify opportunities for growth. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç The trade association are looking for a proactive Events Executive with a strong eye for detail to play a key role in delivering high impact events across the UK. This is an exciting opportunity for someone looking to advance their career in a fast-moving environment. In this role, you will be responsible for supporting end-to-end event planning to create experiences for members which will help strengthen stakeholder and business relationships. This includes supporting the planning and delivery of notable events such as high-level roundtables, conferences, networking opportunities and seminars. The role incorporates the full life cycle of event co-ordination, including concept development, planning and logistics, as well as marketing support and follow-up engagement. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Support the development of event concepts, timelines, budgets, and planning documents to ensure smooth end to end delivery. Coordinate venue bookings, supplier negotiations, and vendor contracts to secure cost effective and high quality event services. Liaise with external partners to ensure all logistics including catering, equipment, and hospitality. Oversee administrative tasks such as delegate registration, speaker coordination, and preparation of event materials and resources. Provide onsite operational support to ensure events run smoothly and meet expected standards. Assist with event communications, including drafting invitations, managing mailing lists, and supporting promotional activities. Support post event follow up, including updates to systems, sending thank you notes, and consolidating feedback from participants. Contribute to post event reporting and evaluations to improve future event delivery and impact. ‚Äç Key Skills and Experience Required ‚Äç Strong written and verbal communication skills and confident in engaging with a range of stakeholders. Strong resilience and time management skills; able to work proactively. Proficient in Canva and Microsoft Office, whilst being comfortable with AI tools ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Salary banding ¬£28,000 - ¬£30,000 dependent on experience Hybrid working arrangement ‚Äì 3 days in the London office ‚Äç How to apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Thursday 7th May 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‚Äç

item.title: Associate Director (Financial Services)

You will play a central role in shaping client strategy, leading delivery across key accounts, and supporting the continued growth of the practice within a nimble and ambitious agency environment. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead the strategic direction and delivery of integrated communications programmes for financial services clients Act as a senior counsel to clients, building trusted and long-term relationships Support and deputise for the Head of Financial Services as required Drive new business activity, including contributing to pitches and identifying growth opportunities Oversee and mentor junior team members, fostering a high-performing and supportive team culture Ensure consistently high standards of delivery across multiple accounts Collaborate across the wider agency to deliver integrated, multi-market campaigns Skills & Experience ‚Äç Significant experience within a communications agency, with a focus on financial services Strong understanding of the financial services landscape, including regulatory and reputational considerations Proven ability to lead client relationships and provide senior strategic counsel Experience in managing and developing high-performing teams Track record of contributing to business development and agency growth Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, agile environment Close ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‚Äç For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Head of Communications
item.description: The organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield are proud to be partnering with the Aerospace Technology Institute (ATI). They are based in London and responsible for the UK‚Äôs Aerospace Technology Strategy. ATI promote transformative technology in air transport, representing a joint industry and Government commitment to R&D funding of over ¬£4 billion by 2031. The ATI represents a single, coherent national aerospace technology development programme that aims to strengthen the links between industry and academia whilst maximising opportunities for UK industry to gain access to European R&T programmes. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç As Head of Communications at the ATI, the successful candidate will direct and elevate the organisation's reach and impact across UK and global audiences, shaping how the world understands ATI's role at the forefront of aerospace technology. ‚Äç Leading the Communications Team, the Head of Communications will own and evolve the full channel mix from media relationships and thought leadership to flagship events including the ATI Annual Conference, while delivering a Communications Plan that brings to life ATI's impact, sector vision, and technology leadership. The Head of Communications will work closely with partners, manage high-profile media engagements, and have regular exposure to the ATI Executive Management Team and Board, with occasional involvement in international events. ‚Äç Reporting to the Chief Relationships Officer, the postholder will directly manage a communications team of 3 and oversee a team of 4, providing strong leadership, clear direction, and a culture of continuous improvement driven by data and feedback. As a member of the Extended Leadership Team, the Head of Communications will collaborate across the ATI to align internal and external messaging, championing ATI's values of Collaborating for Success, Independent Leadership, and Forward Thinking. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Establish and deliver a clear Communications plan that maximises the visibility, impact and reach of ATI content. Deliver an ATI events programme that supports delivery of the ATI funding programme, development of sector competitiveness and growing UK market share by 2035. Transform the delivery of ATI Programme information to ensure portfolio, case study and impact data is shared in a way that maximises accessibility and utility. Work closely with Programme partners, the media and recipients to ensure co-ordinated and timely communications and proactively seek to resolve misunderstanding or misinformation. Provide guidance and content for the delivery of ATI internal communications. Line manage, develop and support the Communications Team, contribute as part of the extended Leadership Team and be a role model in the organisation. ‚Äç Experience required ‚Äç Proven experience running strategic communications campaigns in complex, multi-stakeholder environments, with the ability to structure insights and evidence into compelling narratives quickly and accurately. Demonstrable understanding of the economic and political landscape of the UK aerospace sector or UK Government, with the ability to translate this into strategic opportunities and actionable implications. Experience negotiating and managing contracts, alongside a track record of successfully coordinating multiple delivery partners. Experience working and delivering in multi-stakeholder environments, with the ability to build relationships at all levels and anticipate and respond to a range of perspectives. Ability to create and commission high-quality content and collateral, combined with strong organisational skills to lead and motivate colleagues to deliver to time and budget. Proven experience managing and developing a team, setting clear goals and priorities, fostering a collaborative culture, and driving performance to deliver high-quality outcomes. Direct experience of working in the UK civil aerospace sector, UK Government or a communications agency would be desirable but not essential. ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Hybrid working (2 office days minimum per week in the London office - W1T) Attractive pension scheme Bupa Private Medical Insurance Discretionary bonus scheme Paid membership to one professional body per year 25 days annual leave, plus bank holidays and additional Christmas leave Enhanced maternity and paternity leave EV scheme available ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below by the 19th May, 2026 and include a copy of your CV. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Head of Public Policy & Research
item.description: The organisation ‚Äç People‚Äôs Partnership is a leading financial services organisation, responsible for supporting more than seven million savers across the UK, with over ¬£40bn Assets Under Management (AUM). It plays a significant role in the pensions landscape, working to improve financial outcomes and deliver long-term value for its members. The organisation is recognised for it strong commitment to innovation, customer focus and driving positive change across the industry. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç People‚Äôs Partnership is seeking a Head of Public Policy& Research to lead its thought leadership, research, campaigns and public policy agenda at a senior level. This role will shape how the organisation influences government, industry and key stakeholders, ensuring its voice is clear, credible and impactful across the pensions and financial services landscape. Working closely with senior leadership, including the Head of Public Affairs, Head of Policy and executive team, the Head of Public Policy & Research will set the strategic direction for research, corporate narrative, campaigns and external positioning. ‚Äç ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Lead the development and delivery of a strategic thought leadership and policy research programme aligned to organisational priorities Set and own the corporate policy narrative, ensuring clear and consistent positioning across external policy audiences and internal stakeholders Oversee the commissioning and delivery of high-quality research and insight to support policy development and industry influence Build and maintain senior relationships across industry, government, think tanks and partner organisations Lead and develop a high-performing team, ensuring delivery of impactful campaigns, content and public policy outputs. ‚Äç Key skills and experience ‚Äç Senior experience in policy, research, public affairs or corporate affairs within financial services, government, a think tank or membership organisation Strong track record of developing thought leadership and influencing policy and industry debate Experience commissioning and translating research into clear, compelling narratives for external audiences Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to engage and influence at senior level Strong communication skills, with experience producing high-quality briefings, reports and strategic content. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Application Please apply below by Monday 18th May. ‚Äç

item.title: Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences)
item.description: The organisation: ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to partner with Queen Mary University of London, a prestigious Russell Group University located in the heart of East London. Ranked 31st in the world for the quality of their research, and with a long-standing history of commitment to social justice and academic excellence, Queen Mary is consistently ranked among the top universities in the UK and globally. ‚Äç With a diverse and inclusive community of students and staff from over 170 countries, the University fosters an environment that encourages innovation, collaboration, and critical thinking across a very wide range of disciplines. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity: ‚Äç The role of Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences) sits within the central Communications team and serves as the primary communications lead for the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences. ‚Äç As one of three Faculty Communications Managers‚Äîeach aligned with one of the University‚Äôs faculties‚Äîthis role is responsible for developing and delivering strategic internal and external communications for the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences. Working closely with Faculty leadership, academic and professional services staff, the postholder supports the University‚Äôs broader goals of increasing awareness and reputation, driving student recruitment, and enhancing engagement among staff and students. ‚Äç The Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences) is the lead contact for communications across the Faculty and works in close partnership with the Faculty Marketing Manager to be a point of contact to the central marketing and communications services. The role is supported by the central Communications team and directly line manages a team of two communications officers. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead and deliver integrated internal and external communications strategies for the Faculty, supporting reputation, engagement (internal and external) and student recruitment objectives. Act as the principal communications adviser to Faculty leadership teams, providing expert guidance on media relations, campaigns, stakeholder communications and reputation management. Develop high-quality content across multiple channels, including press releases, digital platforms, social media, newsletters and leadership communications, ensuring consistency of brand and messaging. Monitor, evaluate and report on communications activity and campaign performance, using insight and analytics to inform future strategy and continuous improvement. ‚Äç Key Skills and Experience required: ‚Äç Significant experience of developing and implementing successful communications activities and campaigns (including media relations activities). Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience translating complex subject matter into engaging and accessible content for diverse audiences. Proven experience of coordinating integrated communications across traditional and digital platforms to meet marketing and communications goals. Comfortable working alongside senior leadership teams and or Boards/Committees. Experience of working in within higher education or a complex organisation. Experience of matrix managing and directly line managing others Proven ability to meet tight deadlines ‚Äç Benefits: ‚Äç Salary banding: ¬£54,617 ‚Äì ¬£60,901, dependent on experience* Hybrid working (3 days per week in the office) Office based in London (E1 4UJ) 30 days holiday plus bank holidays *Please note that Queen Mary advertise salaries on a range to indicate the trajectory of progression that can be made. Appointments are usually made at the start of the salary range. The Queen Mary salary structure includes automatic pay progression within the published grades, subject to service, funding and performance. In addition to this, there are performance related annual pay review schemes in place. ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. item.title: Senior Director of Executive Communications
item.description: ‚Äç Role Purpose Lead and shape the executive voice of a global organisation, driving strategic communications and employee engagement across Europe. ‚Äç The Senior Director, Executive Communications is responsible for shaping, protecting and amplifying the voice of the CEO and senior leadership across Europe. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, storytelling, employee engagement and digital communications, translating enterprise priorities into clear, compelling narratives that drive alignment, trust and performance. ‚Äç The role is both strategic and highly operational, owning end to end delivery of executive communications, from narrative development and drafting through to digital execution, sequencing and distribution. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Set and steward the CEO and enterprise narrative, ensuring clarity, consistency and relevance across all channels and moments that matter. Draft and edit high‚Äëstakes communications on behalf of the CEO and ELT, including organisational announcements, leadership notes, speeches, townhalls, blogs and event messaging. Act as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, providing counsel on tone, timing, positioning and stakeholder impact. Lead enterprise‚Äëwide employee communications, translating strategy, transformation and performance into messages that resonate with colleagues. Own leadership‚Äëled employee moments such as town halls, leadership updates and milestone communications, ensuring they are engaging, data‚Äëdriven and authentic. Partner closely with People, Strategy and Leadership teams to reinforce culture, purpose and change. Drive a digital‚Äëfirst approach to executive and employee communications, using platforms such as Engage, intranet, video and social to extend reach and relevance. Modernise executive communications through smart use of visuals, links, data, multimedia and new formats, ensuring content is concise, scannable and shareable. Ensure messages are adapted appropriately for different channels while maintaining a single narrative spine. Own the end‚Äëto‚Äëend communications flow for executive and enterprise priorities, including sequencing, timing and coordination across internal, external and digital channels. Work seamlessly with Corporate, Product and Market Communications teams to ensure alignment and avoid fragmentation. Partner with global counterparts to localise and execute global narratives effectively in Europe. Act as a final editorial gatekeeper for executive communications, ensuring accuracy, consistency and readiness before distribution. Manage sensitive, confidential communications with discretion and sound judgement. Anticipate reputational risk and advise on mitigation through proactive messaging. Key Skills and Experience ‚Äç Extensive experience in executive, employee or enterprise communications within a complex, matrixed organisation. Exceptional writing and editing skills, with the ability to shift seamlessly between strategic narrative and sharp, concise execution. Strong understanding of digital communications and channel strategy, with a track record of modernising leadership communications. High emotional intelligence and confidence operating with senior leaders. Proven ability to manage multiple high‚Äëstakes workstreams under pressure without losing quality or judgement. ‚Äç Application ‚Äç To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com. ‚Äç

item.title: Policy and Public Affairs Lead (Wales)
item.description: The organisation Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with a leading health charitythat plays a prominent role in shaping policy and delivering high-profilecampaigns to improve access to diagnosis, treatment and support for patientsacross the UK. The opportunity Our client is looking for an experienced Policy and Public Affairs Lead to leadthe development and communication of evidence-based policy positions acrossWales. In this role, you will play a central part in shaping and deliveringinfluencing strategies, while building and maintaining strong relationshipswith stakeholders across the Welsh Government and wider health sector. You will work closely with researchers, healthcareprofessionals and external partners to amplify policy and campaign activity,ensuring the charity‚Äôs priorities are informed by robust evidence and capableof driving meaningful change. This is an exciting opportunity to join a highlyregarded organisation at a time of significant policy focus and campaigningactivity across Wales. ‚Äç Main responsibilities Work closely with colleagues across the organisation to ensure alignment on policy and campaign priorities. Draft high-quality policy materials including briefings, consultation responses, reports and other written outputs on key policy areas. Ensure policy development is backed by a strong evidence base, informed by healthcare professionals and patient insight across Wales. Monitor the political and healthcare landscape in Wales to identify emerging risks, opportunities and influencing priorities. Collaborate with external organisations on areas of mutual interest to maximise campaign reach and impact. Work closely with public affairs colleagues to identify and deliver activities that support the charity‚Äôs influencing objectives. Build and maintain effective relationships with policymakers, healthcare leaders, clinicians and external partners across Wales. Represent the charity at key events and conferences, acting as a source of expertise on the Welsh political and healthcare landscape. Support the press and PR team to ensure policy and public affairs priorities are reflected in media activity and opportunities. ‚Äç Key skills and experience required Experience developing and implementing policy and influencing strategies within a Welsh political environment. Strong ability to utilise data and evidence, including patient insight, to support policy development and influencing activity. Proven track record of delivering public affairs activity across Wales, with the ability to influence senior external stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to represent the charity externally and communicate complex policy issues clearly. Strong project management skills, with the ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities and stakeholders. ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Salary banding between ¬£36,500 - ¬£38,000 Hybrid working, with 3 days per week in the Cardiff office 25 days annual leave, plus an additional day for each full year worked (up to 30 days) ‚Äç How to apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link below. ‚Äç The deadline for applications is Tuesday 26th May 2026. However, we may close the advert earlier if we receive a high volume of applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‚Äç

item.title: Graduate Communications Intern (6-month FTC)
item.description: The Opportunity: ‚Äç We are partnering with a mission driven organisation in the insurance industry to recruit a Graduate Communications Intern on a 6-month FTC. This is a great opportunity to immerse yourself in a highly collaborative team dedicated to addressing critical challenges around risk management. This full-time internship offers hands-on experience in internal, corporate and social and digital communications, in a purpose driven organisation. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Accountabilities: ‚Äç Collaborate with the communications team to develop and deliver a new internal communications strategy. Support planning and execution of internal events. Maintain the corporate calendar, including ensuring Chair, CEO and Exec speaking engagements are captured and advise the team when they need to prepare speeches and briefings. Collate content for monthly round-up emails. Produce content for social and digital channels Lead on admin responsibilities for the communications team ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Experience Required: ‚Äç Great Writing Skills: Contribute to internal announcements, blogs, and corporate content with clarity and impact. Excellent Interpersonal and Communication Skills: Collaborate effectively with team members and stakeholders while maintaining professionalism. Detail-Oriented and Organised: From managing SharePoint pages to tracking events, demonstrate your ability to juggle multiple responsibilities seamlessly. Adaptability: Embrace changing technologies and business needs with confidence and resourcefulness. Proactive Problem Solver: Take initiative and work independently while integrating seamlessly into our client‚Äôs collaborative environment. Experience producing content for Social and Digital channels ‚Äç ‚Äç If this role looks of interest, please apply through the link in this advert and not via email. ‚Äç

item.title: Senior Policy Manager
item.description: The organisation ‚Äç London & Partners is the growth agency for London, working to support the city‚Äôs economy, attract investment, drive innovation and help businesses scale. Working across high-growth sectors and in partnership with business, government and industry, London & Partners plays a central role in promoting London as a leading global city for investment, talent and innovation. Its work focuses on building a more sustainable, resilient and inclusive economy for the capital. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç London & Partners is seeking a Senior Policy Manager to play a leading role in shaping policy and thought leadership focused on London‚Äôs economic growth. Sitting within the Policy and Impact Unit, the Senior Policy Manager will develop insight-led policy ideas and market interventions that support growth sectors, innovation and business development across the capital. The role offers the opportunity to work on high-profile projects spanning areas such as innovation, climate, life sciences, AI and business growth, while engaging closely with senior stakeholders across government, industry and the wider London ecosystem. ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Develop policy insights, thought leadership and market-focused recommendations to support London‚Äôs economic growth and high-growth sectors Lead and manage complex policy and strategy projects, ensuring delivery to agreed time, budget and quality standards Analyse economic, sector and policy trends to identify opportunities, barriers and practical solutions Build and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders across government, business, think tanks and academia Produce high-quality briefings, reports and presentations, and represent London & Partners at meetings, events and stakeholder forums. ‚Äç Key skills and experience ‚Äç Strong experience in economic growth, innovation or sector-focused policy roles within government, a thinktank, business organisation or related environment Proven ability to develop influential policy ideas, thought leadership and evidence-based recommendations Excellent project management skills, with experience leading complex programmes and coordinating multiple stakeholders Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly for different audiences Experience building relationships with senior stakeholders across policy, government and industry. ‚Äç Application ‚Äç To apply, please share your CV using the link below before the closing deadline on Wednesday 27 th May 2026. ‚Äç

item.title: Content and Digital Communications Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‚Äç Lead the voice of a major UK digital infrastructure business, shaping high-impact content and social storytelling that drives awareness, engagement, and commercial growth. ‚Äç If you are energised by building brands through compelling storytelling, strategic content, and high-performing social channels, this role offers the opportunity to make a visible impact. This organisation is at the forefront of UK digital infrastructure, delivering connectivity solutions that support customers, landlords, and partners across the telecoms ecosystem. ‚Äç As Content & Digital Communications Lead, you will own and evolve the organisation‚Äôs content marketing and organic social strategy, helping shape how the business communicates across digital channels. You will play a central role in building a scalable always-on content engine that strengthens brand credibility, increases engagement, and supports measurable demand creation across both the core business and new growth propositions. ‚Äç This is a highly collaborative and fast-moving environment where creativity, strategic thinking, and strong execution are genuinely valued. You will work closely with senior stakeholders, support executive visibility, and create content that influences audiences across the industry. ‚Äç ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Own the organisation‚Äôs LinkedIn and organic social strategy, driving corporate visibility, thought leadership, proposition education, and growth campaign engagement. Build and manage an always-on social publishing engine, including content series, campaign amplification, publishing cadence, and community engagement strategy. Create high-performing digital content optimised for LinkedIn and other social platforms, using strong storytelling, audience insight, and clear calls-to-action. Support CEO and senior leadership visibility through executive content drafting, voice adaptation, and strategic narrative alignment. Partner closely with Studio, Corporate Communications, and wider marketing teams to deliver integrated multi-channel campaigns and standout creative content. Monitor and optimise social and content performance, using insight and analytics to continuously improve engagement, audience growth, and downstream campaign impact. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Key skills and experience required ‚Äç Deep expertise in LinkedIn, B2B digital content marketing, and organic social growth strategies. Strong understanding of content performance, audience engagement, and algorithm-informed publishing best practice. Exceptional copywriting and storytelling skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics into engaging and commercially impactful content. Experience building scalable content programmes, repeatable publishing rhythms, and executive thought leadership content. Proven success growing LinkedIn engagement and visibility for B2B brands and/or senior leadership teams.. Experience within telecoms, digital infrastructure, or closely related sectors is highly desirable. Ability to work collaboratively across marketing, communications, and senior stakeholder groups in a fast-paced environment. ‚Äç Application ‚Äç To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç

item.title: Brand Marketing Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‚Äç Drive the brand strategy of a leading UK digital infrastructure business, shaping high-impact campaigns that build market credibility, awareness, and commercial growth If you are excited by the opportunity to shape brand strategy, launch impactful campaigns, and position a business at the centre of a rapidly evolving industry, this role offers the chance to make a visible and lasting impact. This organisation operates at the forefront of UK digital infrastructure, delivering connectivity solutions that support customers, landlords, and partners across the telecoms ecosystem. ‚Äç As Brand Marketing Manager, you will lead the organisation‚Äôs brand marketing strategy and execution, building awareness, credibility, and market preference across priority audiences. You will create and deliver top and mid-funnel campaigns that support new products, vertical market expansion, and long-term brand growth across both the core business and new business acceleration initiatives. ‚Äç This is a highly collaborative role where you will work closely with Product, Sales, Performance Marketing, Content, and Creative teams to bring brand narratives to life across multiple channels. You will play a key role in establishing market positioning, strengthening category leadership, and building the foundation for measurable commercial growth. ‚Äç ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Own the organisation‚Äôs brand marketing strategy, roadmap, and execution aligned to strategic priorities, core business growth, and new market expansion. Lead top and mid-funnel campaign strategy and delivery across new propositions, vertical markets, enterprise segments, and category positioning initiatives. Translate brand strategy and value propositions into compelling messaging frameworks, campaign territories, and evidence-led storytelling. Deliver integrated brand-led campaigns that drive awareness and consideration across digital, trade, paid, and earned channels. Own always-on brand activity including thought leadership, market education, and credibility-building campaigns. Partner closely with Product, Growth, Sales, Content, and Creative teams to ensure alignment between brand messaging and commercial priorities. Support campaign execution through clear briefing, stakeholder management, and collaboration with Brand & Creative Studio teams. Track and report on brand and upper-funnel performance including awareness, engagement, message cut-through, and share of voice. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Key skills and experience required ‚Äç Strong expertise in B2B brand marketing and upper-funnel campaign strategy. Proven ability to build awareness and consideration campaigns for complex products, propositions, or technical services. Strong narrative development, positioning, and messaging capability. Experience launching new products, propositions, or vertical market campaigns. Understanding of how brand and upper-funnel activity supports down streamdemand generation and lead conversion. Experience within telecoms, digital infrastructure, or closely related sectors is highly desirable. Strong stakeholder management, prioritisation, and campaign delivery skills within fast-paced environments. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Application ‚Äç To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com.

item.title: Director of Communications
item.description: Ellwood Atfield are proud to be partnering with a leading public sector regulatory body that plays a vital role in upholding information rights and data protection standards across the UK. ‚Äç ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç This is a rare opportunity for a senior communications leader to join an organisation at a pivotal moment and shape the way it communicates its purpose, decisions and regulatory authority to a wide range of stakeholders. ‚Äç Reporting to the Executive Director of Communications and Public Affairs and as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director of Communications will set the vision and strategic direction for a modern, high-performing communications function that is embedded early in organisational decision-making and will be leading a multidisciplinary team supported by two direct reports and oversee a team of30. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Set and lead the communications strategy, aligned to organisational priorities and regulatory objectives, working in close partnership with the Executive Director. Position communications as a core strategic function, embedded early in planning, policy and regulatory decision making.. Provide senior counsel to the Executive Team on sensitive, high-profile and reputationally significant issues, leading the organisation‚Äôs approach to reputation management and crisis readiness. Champion an insight-driven, outcomes-focused approach to communications, using research, data and evaluation to inform strategy and measure impact. Lead a digital first culture, ensuring channels, content and formats reflect how audiences consume information, while encouraging creativity and innovation. Own and shape the organisation‚Äôs overarching narrative and lead proactive engagement to build public trust and confidence. Work with internal communications to align internal and external messaging, supporting leadership communication capability and consistency across the organisation. Lead, inspire and develop a collaborative, high-performing team of around 30 people, fostering a culture of confidence, clarity, accountability and continuous improvement. ‚Äç ‚Äç Experience Required ‚Äç Senior leadership experience running a substantial communications function in a high profile, scrutinised environment. Proven experience advising senior leaders on sensitive, complex and reputationally significant communications issues. A demonstrable record of using communications to shape outcomes Experience setting and delivering communications strategy across multiple disciplines, including media, digital, campaigns, stakeholder engagement and events. Strong track record of leading and developing senior managers and building high-performing, confident teams. Experience managing resources and budgets, including commissioning and working with agencies to enhance capacity and capability. Deep understanding of insight-driven, audience-led communications, including digital first approaches and modern communications practice. ‚Äç ‚Äç How to apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. item.title: Chief Executive Officer
item.description: The opportunity: ‚Äç The British Compressed Gases Association (BCGA) is the leading UK trade association representing the interests of 100+ member companies. These companies, in the industrial, food and medical gases industries, include manufacturers and suppliers of liquefied, dissolved and compressed gases as well as the associated gaseous pressure systems, pipework, components and related equipment. ‚Äç Members also provide specialist safety, health, environment, quality, inspection and training services. Today, BCGA members employ over 19,000 people directly and account for an annual turnover of over ¬£2.7 bn. ‚Äç The new Chief Executive Officer will lead BCGA and deliver its mission as the UK‚Äôs authoritative body for safety, standards and best practice across the industrial and medical gases sector. ‚Äç ‚Äç Responsibilities include: ‚Äç Setting and deliver BCGA‚Äôs strategic direction, ensuring the Association remains relevant, influential and responsive to sector priorities. Leading effective operational delivery, financial stewardship and robust governance in partnership with the BCGA Council. Safeguarding and strengthening BCGA‚Äôs reputation as a trusted technical authority on safety, standards and best practice. Representing BCGA and the wider compressed gases sector with credibility, impartiality and integrity to members, regulators, government and industry stakeholders. ‚Äç Requirements include: ‚Äç Senior leadership experience, ideally gained in a complex, technical, regulated or membership-based organisation. Experience of operating successfully in environments where safety, standards, governance and stakeholder confidence are critical. Experience engaging with government, regulators, industry bodies or professional institutions. Awareness of the challenges and opportunities facing technical, regulated and safety-critical sectors. ‚Äç ‚Äç This exciting opportunity is open to candidates with the credibility and judgement to step into a high-profile leadership role, whether as an experienced CEO or a senior leader ready to take on a first CEO appointment. ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç For more information, please consult the detailed candidate pack and apply with a supporting statement and CV via the form below. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Policy Officer
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of two Senior Policy Officers. ‚Äç The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater external impact across the issues affecting surgeons and patients. ‚Äç Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in shaping standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and influencing debate across major healthcare and workforce challenges. ‚Äç ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç This is an excellent opportunity for a policy professional who enjoys combining analytical thinking with practical, outcomes-focused policy development. ‚Äç As Senior Policy Officer, you will support the delivery of the College‚Äôs policy objectives across key issues affecting surgical practice, workforce and healthcare delivery. Working closely with clinicians and colleagues across policy, campaigns, publishing, and communications, you will help develop evidence-based policy positions designed to influence government, regulators and NHS bodies. ‚Äç You will also play an important role in horizon scanning, identifying emerging political, healthcare and regulatory developments, and ensuring the College remains a credible and influential voice on issues impacting surgery and patient care. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Develop evidence-led policy positions across issues impacting surgical practice, workforce and standards. Produce high-quality policy briefings, consultation responses and stakeholder briefings and engagement materials. Monitor political, NHS and regulatory developments to identify emerging risks and influencing opportunities. Work closely with campaigns, communications and public affairs colleagues to support wider influencing objectives. Build strong relationships with clinicians, healthcare organisations, regulators and government stakeholders. Support senior stakeholders and College spokespeople with policy advice and external engagement. ‚Äç ‚Äç Person specification ‚Äç Develop Experience working within policy, public affairs or influencing roles in healthcare, government, regulation or membership organisations. Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate complex information into practical policy recommendations. Excellent written communication skills and experience producing materials for senior audiences. Understanding of political, healthcare or regulatory environments. Strong stakeholder engagement skills and ability to work collaboratively across teams. Comfortable working at pace and managing competing objectives. ‚Äç ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: 9:00am Friday 12th June Interview date: Week commencing 22nd June We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‚Äç ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç

item.title: Policy Officer
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment a Policy Officers. ‚Äç The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater external impact across the issues affecting surgeons and patients. ‚Äç Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in shaping standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and influencing debate across major healthcare and workforce challenges. ‚Äç ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a career in health policy and influencing while helping shape the future direction of surgical policy and influencing work at one of the UK‚Äôs most respected professional bodies. ‚Äç As Policy Officer, you will support the development and delivery of policy activity across a wide range of issues affecting surgical practice, workforce and healthcare delivery. Working closely with clinicians and colleagues across policy, campaigns, publications, and communications, you will monitor and analyse political, NHS and regulatory developments, identify emerging trends and support the development of accessible policy insights and member communications. ‚Äç The successful candidate will join a collaborative and ambitious team environment with the opportunity to help shape the development of surgical policy, campaigns and member engagement work at one of the UK‚Äôs leading professional membership organisations. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Monitor political, NHS and regulatory developments relevant to surgery and dental surgery, identifying emerging issues and opportunities. Produce high-quality concise policy updates, briefings and analysis for colleagues, members and stakeholders. item.title: Engagement Lead (Higher Education)
item.description: About the organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is delighted to have partnered with a respected trade association who are looking for an Engagement Lead on a permanent basis. The organisation is renowned for working alongside Government and business leaders to provide insights and advice around securing investment opportunities in Asia. ‚Äç The organisation work closely with its members to help businesses influence policy, connect with decision-makers and identify opportunities for growth. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç A key element to the organisation‚Äôs growth strategy lies within identifying and leveraging opportunities within the Higher Education sector. As an Engagement Lead, you will play a crucial part in driving expansion; operating as a visible leader for UK university clients. You will optimise brand presence throughout the sector, through partnerships, policy influence and market knowledge. You will also manage a broad range of relationships, engaging with policy teams relating to international student recruitment, cross University partnerships as well as market entry for UK universities. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Establish and maintain client relationships across the higher education sector through proactive engagement. Liaise with clients to understand their issues and strategic needs pertaining to cross country University relations. Promote awareness of launchpad and other services provided by the trade association within the higher education sector. Optimise driving client retention and acquisition through innovative recruitment strategies and market insights. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Skills and Experience Required ‚Äç Extensive experience in UK Higher Education (preferably in international offices) student recruitment, or partnerships. Strong understanding of sector challenges and solutions for growth in fast-evolving landscapes. Proven ability to quickly build trust withsenior external stakeholders including University Vice-Chancellors and Deans. Able to manage various aspects of stakeholder and client relationships including lead generation, pitching, closure and delivery oversight. Good understanding of HE decision-making as well as student flows and visa compliance obstacles. Adept in conducting qualitative/quantitative research to inform policy reports/briefings on HE trends, and sector reforms. Confident representing the organisation at external events. ‚Äç

item.title: Integrated Communications Manager
item.description: Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with a globally respected membership body at the forefront of maritime innovation to recruit anIntegrated Communications Manager. ‚Äç For more than 160 years, the Royal Institute of Naval Architects (RINA) has advanced the art and science of naval architecture through technical excellence, thought leadership and global collaboration. Today, it brings together professionals across more than 140 countries working across everything from green propulsion and AI to warship resilience and autonomy. ‚Äç This is a newly created role at the heart of a fast-moving marketing and communications team during a significant period of organisational transformation and growth. ‚Äç The Integrated Communications Manager will lead integrated email marketing, CRM-led communications and cross-organisational campaign planning -helping grow membership, increase readership of The Naval Architect , expand specialist newsletter audiences and support wider business development activity. ‚Äç This is not a traditional hard-sell marketing role. RINA‚Äôs approach is ‚Äúhelpful first, selling second‚Äù: using high-quality content, audience insight and carefully targeted communications to build trust, engagement and long-term member value. ‚Äç The successful candidate will combine strong copywriting and campaign instincts with hands-on experience across CRM and email marketing. Maritime experience is not required, but curiosity, commercial instinct and an ability to engage credibly with technical subject matter are essential. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key responsibilities include: ‚Äç ‚Äç Leading integrated email marketing andcommunications campaigns across membership, publications and businessdevelopment Managing CRM segmentation, automation andaudience targeting Creating and overseeing an organisation-wideemail communications calendar Supporting cross-sell and upsell campaigns andconversion journeys Working closely with content, events andpublications colleagues to maximise audience engagement Tracking campaign performance and usinginsight to improve effectiveness and conversion ‚Äç ‚Äç We are particularly interested in speaking with candidates fromintegrated communications, CRM, membership marketing, publishing, subscriptionor B2B content-led environments. ‚Äç RINA offers a highly flexible and collaborative working culture,with significant scope for creativity, experimentation and visible impact. ‚Äç Closing date: Friday 12 June Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so earlyapplications are encouraged. We will only be able to considerapplications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‚Äç ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs,communications, policy, and advocacy.
