item.title: Industry Relations Manager
item.description: About the organisation: ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to be supporting a nationally recognised organisation at the forefront of one of the UK‚Äôs largest infrastructure and consumer engagement programmes. The Opportunity: ‚Äç This is a highly collaborative and externally focused role within the Corporate Affairs function, offering the opportunity to work closely with senior stakeholders across industry, regulation and partner organisations. ‚Äç The Industry Relations Manager will lead engagement activity with key industry partners, providing insight, coordination and strategic support across a range of programmes and initiatives. You will monitor sector developments, identify implications for stakeholders and support the delivery of collaborative projects that contribute to long-term organisational objectives. ‚Äç This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys building relationships, navigating complex stakeholder environments, and translating technical or policy information into clear, actionable insight. Main responsibilities: ‚Äç Build and maintain strong relationships with industry stakeholders, acting as a trusted point of contact. Support the development and delivery of stakeholder engagement strategies and collaborative projects. Monitor sector, regulatory and policy developments, identifying potential risks and opportunities. Produce clear briefings, presentations and insight to support internal decision-making. Work cross-functionally with communications, insights and corporate affairs teams to align stakeholder activity. Represent the organisation at external meetings, forums and industry events. Support and develop junior colleagues, helping ensure high-quality delivery across the function. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key skills and experience required: ‚Äç Experience delivering stakeholder engagement or industry relations activity within a complex environment. Strong relationship management skills, with the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including presenting complex information clearly. Strong organisational and project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Ability to interpret policy, regulatory or operational developments and communicate implications effectively. item.title: Head of External Affairs
item.description: ‚Äç The organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with The Health Foundation on the appointment of a Head of External Affairs. ‚Äç The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‚Äç At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç This is a high-profile opportunity to lead external affairs, media relations and stakeholder engagement for one of the UK‚Äôs most respected and influential health policy organisations. ‚Äç Reporting into the Deputy Director of Communications, the Head of External Affairs will lead a busy and high-performing team responsible for media, public affairs and stakeholder relations activity across the Foundation‚Äôs broad portfolio of work. ‚Äç The successful candidate will play a central role in shaping and delivering the Foundation‚Äôs influencing strategy - identifying opportunities to position the organisation at the forefront of political, policy and public debate across health and care. ‚Äç Working closely with senior leadership, the role will provide strategic counsel across media, government relations, reputational issues and stakeholder engagement, while helping ensure the Foundation‚Äôs work reaches and influences key audiences nationally. ‚Äç This role would suit an experienced communications, public affairs or external affairs leader who combines strong political and policy awareness with excellent judgement, leadership capability and highly developed stakeholder management skills. ‚Äç Key responsibilities ‚Äç Lead the Foundation‚Äôs external affairs, media relations and influencing activity across health and public policy issues. Develop high-profile and timely opportunities to position the Foundation at the centre of policy, political and media debate. Oversee engagement with senior stakeholders across Westminster, Whitehall, media and the wider health sector. Lead and develop the Foundation‚Äôs media relations strategy, ensuring strong profile and impactful coverage across target audiences. Provide strategic communications and public affairs advice to senior leaders and colleagues across the organisation. Develop compelling messaging, briefings and external affairs materials for a range of audiences. Lead, motivate and develop ahigh-performing external affairs team. Contribute to the wider leadership of the Communications function as a senior member of the management team. ‚Äç ‚Äç Person specification ‚Äç Significant experience leading external affairs, media, communications or public affairs functions within complex organisations. Strong understanding of UK politics, public policy and influencing environments. Demonstrable track record of delivering profile, impact and organisational influence through integrated external affairs strategies. Excellent stakeholder management skills with the ability to build credibility at senior levels. Highly accomplished written and verbal communication skills. Experience leading and developing teams within fast-paced and high-profile environments. Ability to manage complex issues with sound judgement, discretion and political awareness. Interest in health policy, public policy and mission-led work. ‚Äç ‚Äç How to apply ‚Äç For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‚Äç To access the job description, please click here. ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‚Äç Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‚Äç We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. item.description: ‚Äç ‚Äç The organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Health Foundation on the appointment of a Deputy Director of Communications. ‚Äç The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‚Äç At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç This is a significant senior leadership opportunity within one of the UK‚Äôs most respectedand influential health policy organisations. ‚Äç Reporting to the Director of Communications, the Deputy Director of Communications will play a central role in shaping and delivering integrated communications strategies across the Foundation‚Äôs major thematic programmes of work. ‚Äç The successful candidate will work closely with senior leadership across the organisation to ensure communications activity delivers impact, strengthens organisational influence and supports the Foundation‚Äôs ambition to be one of the leading voices in UK public policy. ‚Äç The remit is broad and highly strategic - spanning communications strategy, stakeholder engagement, external affairs, reputation, thought leadership and executive counsel. The role also sits on the Foundation‚Äôs Senior Leadership Team and plays an important role in the wider leadership of the Communications directorate. ‚Äç This opportunity would suit an experienced strategic communications leader with strong policy instincts, excellent leadership capability and experience operating within complex, high-profile or mission-led organisations. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key responsibilities ‚Äç Lead integrated communications strategies across major thematic programmes of work. Strong written and verbal communication capability, including experience handling complex and sensitive issues. Experience advising senior leaders and operating at executive level. Leadership experience within fast-paced, high-profile or mission-led environments. Interest in health policy, health care and improving public outcomes. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç How to apply ‚Äç For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‚Äç To access the job description, please click here. ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‚Äç Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‚Äç We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. item.title: Senior Campaigns Officer
item.description: ‚Äç The organisation Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of two Senior Campaigns Officers. ‚Äç The Royal College of Surgeons of England is one of the world‚Äôs leading professional surgical membership organisations, advancing surgical standards and improving patient care across the UK and internationally. ‚Äç The College is entering an exciting new chapter, launching a new corporate strategy alongside newly structured Policy and Campaigns functions designed to deliver more focused, impactful and outcomes-driven influencing activity. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç This is an exciting opportunity to help deliver high-impact campaigns focused on improving surgical care, influencing health policy, and driving meaningful change for surgeons and patients. ‚Äç Working closely with the Campaigns Manager and colleagues across policy, communications and public affairs, the Senior Campaigns Officer will support the delivery of integrated campaigns designed to influence governments, NHS bodies, regulators and wider healthcare stakeholders. ‚Äç The role will suit someone with strong political awareness, excellent communication skills and the ability to translate complex policy issues into compelling campaign activity. You will thrive in a fast-paced environment, spotting opportunities to influence debate and helping deliver campaigns that achieve real-world impact. ‚Äç Please also click here to see the applicant pack and introductory letter from Patrick Leahy, Director of Communications, outlining the College‚Äôs wider vision for the new Policy and Campaigns function and the opportunity these roles present. ‚Äç ‚Äç Responsibilities ‚Äç Support the planning and delivery of high-impact campaigns aligned to the College‚Äôs influencing priorities. Translate policy, evidence and insight into compelling campaign messaging and calls to action. Work collaboratively with policy, communications and public affairs colleagues to deliver integrated influencing activity. Support member engagement and mobilisation activity where appropriate. Monitor political, policy and media developments, identifying opportunities to advance the College‚Äôs position. Build productive relationships with external stakeholders and partner organisations. Use insight, evaluation and campaign performance data to support continuous improvement. ‚Äç ‚Äç Person specification ‚Äç Experience delivering campaigns, advocacy or influencing activity within policy, public affairs, healthcare, charities, membership bodies or related environments. Collaborative and confident working style with strong stakeholder engagement skills. item.title: Regulatory Affairs Lead
item.description: ‚Äç The organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield are proud to partner with Exchange for Change, the not-for-profit organisation appointed by the UK government to design, build and operate the UK's new Deposit Return Scheme (DRS) for single-use drinks containers made from PET plastic, steel and aluminium. ‚Äç This scheme which launches on 1st October 2027 across England, Scotland, Northern Ireland will be the world‚Äôs biggest DRS and one of the most ambitious environmental infrastructure programmes in the UK‚Äôs history. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç Exchange for Change is seeking a Regulatory Affairs Lead to take ownership of the organisation's regulatory strategy, ensuring full compliance with DRS legislation and all statutory obligations. The role will be responsible for developing and executing a robust regulatory plan, while proactively monitoring the external environment to anticipate change and futureproof both Exchange for Change and the DRS. ‚Äç ‚Äç Central to the position is the ability to build and maintain strong, collaborative relationships with both with regulators and internal stakeholders, to drive engagement, confidence and transparency. The Regulatory Affairs Lead will play a key role in upholding the highest standards of governance and accountability, ensuring Exchange for Change operates with integrity at every level as it delivers one of the most significant environmental programmes in UK history. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Develop and implement Exchange for Change's regulatory strategy, ensuring full compliance with UK DRS legislation while proactively monitoring the policy landscape to identify, assess and mitigate risks, and futureproof the organisation and the scheme. Lead Exchange for Change's input into external policy and regulatory consultations, representing the organisation at key stakeholder forums and shaping regulatory activity in line with strategic priorities. Act as the strategic lead for all regulator relationships, convening and directing cross-functional teams to deliver tailored engagement strategies across each nation, ensuring consistent messaging, coordinated approaches and effective intelligence-sharing. Develop and implement a proactive regulatory affairs engagement strategy, collaborating closely with the Head of External Affairs to align and streamline engagement with both regulators and government representatives. Oversee all regulatory reporting and compliance submissions, including quarterly compliance reports, annual funding reports and operational plans for England, Northern Ireland and Scotland. Recruit and line manage a Regulatory Affairs Manager, fostering a culture of collaboration, high performance and shared accountability across the regulatory affairs function. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Experience required ‚Äç Extensive senior level experience in leading regulatory affairs, compliance or public affairs within a multi-stakeholder and matrixed organisation including FMCG, utilities, transport, logistics and environmental management. Strong experience in working with government departments, regulators and trade associations. Proven capability engaging diverse stakeholders across industry, supply chains, regulators and government. Strong understanding of devolved regulatory systems and differing policy/legislative contexts in England, Scotland and Northern Ireland. Strategic thinker who can assess, manage and mitigate sensitive issues/risks, balancing political, operational and commercial considerations effectively. Exceptional communication skills, capable of translating complex operational issues for political, industry and internal audiences. Understanding of UK environmental and waste management regulation would be beneficial. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç 34 days holiday (Including bank holidays) Car salary sacrifice scheme ‚Äç ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below by Monday 15th June, 2026 and include a copy of your CV. ‚Äç ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Communications Executive
item.description: ‚Äç About the organisation: ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield are proud to be supporting a leading transport and logistics company to recruit Communications Executive. The opportunity: ‚Äç Working as part of a collaborative communications team, you will support the planning, creation and delivery of content across a variety of channels. You will help ensure communications are clear, consistent and aligned with organisational priorities while contributing to initiatives that strengthen reputation and employee engagement. ‚Äç The successful candidate will be a strong communicator with excellent writing skills and the ability to tailor your approach for different audiences. You'll be organised, proactive and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. ‚Äç Regular travel across the UK will be required to engage with colleagues, gather content and support communications activity across multiple locations. ‚Äç Main responsibilities: ‚Äç Create and deliver engaging content across internal and external communications channels, including digital, social media and corporate platforms. Support the planning, coordination and delivery of communications campaigns and activities aligned to organisational priorities. Build relationships with colleagues across the business, travelling regularly across the UK to gather content, develop stories and support stakeholder engagement. Monitor media coverage and communications performance, providing insight and reporting to support informed decision-making. Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external partners to ensure consistent messaging and the timely delivery of communications materials, briefings and updates. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Key skills and experience required: ‚Äç Experience in communications, public relations, journalism, marketing or a related field. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Experience creating content for digital and social media channels. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Professional judgement and discretion when handling sensitive information. Willingness and ability to travel regularly across the UK. ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‚Äç Application deadline: 9am on 17 th June ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
